session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9365 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified PR number.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Classify PR review feedback by filling in semantic fields from mechanical CLI output. 16→ 17→## Steps 18→ 19→1. **Run mechanical classification:** 20→ 21→ ```bash 22→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback [--pr <number>] [--include-resolved] 23→ ``` 24→ 25→ Pass `--pr <number>` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. Pass `--include-resolved` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. 26→ 27→ This returns JSON with mechanically pre-classified items: 28→ - **review_submissions**: PR-level reviews (classification: "actionable" | "informational" | "needs_llm") 29→ - **review_threads**: Inline review threads (pre_existing_candidate flag set for bot + restructured) 30→ - **discussion_comments**: Discussion comments (classification: "informational" | "needs_llm")
... [169 lines omitted] ...
104→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 106→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 144→ "error": null 159→## Error Case 173→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 191→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the classify-pr-feedback command with PR 9365:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run mechanical classification of PR feedback

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9365, "pr_title": "Phase 3: Rename plan_store/plan_backend properties and helper functions to pr_store/pr_backend", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9365", "review_submissions": [], "review_threads": [ { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc515MA1", "path": "docs/learned/glossary.md", "line": 512, "is_outdated": false, "author": "github-actions", "comment_preview": "**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`pr_store: PrStore` - Plan storage operations"
Reality: The field is typed as `ManagedPrBackend`, not `PrStore`. There is no `PrStore` type in th", "is_bot": false, "pre_existing_candidate": false } ], "discussion_comments": [ { "comment_id": 4103595459, "author": "schrockn", "body_preview": "Plan Queued for Implementation

session.tool_result: Phase 3: Rename plan_store/plan_backend properties and helper functions to pr_store/pr_backend https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9365
session.assistant: Now I'll get the full content of the review threads and discussion comments to provide complete semantic classification:
Let me get the full review thread and discussion comments:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: gh pr view 9365 --json comments --jq '.comments[] | {id, author: .author.login, isMinimized, body: .body[0:300]}'

session.assistant: Let me get the review thread comment from the line in glossary.md:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: gh api repos/dagster-io/erk/pulls/9365/comments --jq '.[] | select(.path == "docs/learned/glossary.md") | {id, thread_id: .id, path, line, body}'

session.tool_result:
{"body":"**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`pr_store: PrStore` - Plan storage operations"
Reality: The field is typed as `ManagedPrBackend`, not `PrStore`. There is no `PrStore` type in the codebase — `grep -rn 'PrStore' packages/erk-shared/src/` returns no results. The actual declaration is `pr_store: ManagedPrBackend` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py:90`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py:90`

Suggested fix: Change to ```pr_store: ManagedPrBackend``` - Plan storage operations","id":2969754408,"line":512,"path":"docs/learned/glossary.md","thread_id":2969754408}
